messages.content: I found a job on LinkedIn I want to track. How do I add it to my board?

Click "＋ Add role" in the header — it opens the "Add a role" modal. Enter the company and job title (both required) and, if you have it, paste the posting link under "Link to the posting" — optional but recommended. Click "Add to board"; the button reads "Adding…" while it saves. The role lands in "Sourced" and its panel opens automatically.
I clicked Add to board but nothing happened — what's wrong?

If company or job title is blank, the modal shows "Company and job title are required." and won't save. Fill in both fields — the posting link is optional — then click "Add to board" again. Once it saves, the button briefly reads "Adding…" and the board reloads with the new role's panel open.
Does CareerOps figure out the seniority level when I add a role manually?

Yes. When you add a role, CareerOps derives a level from the job title text: "VP," "vice president," or "chief" maps to "VP"; "senior director" maps to "Senior Director"; "principal" maps to "Principal"; "director" maps to "Director"; anything else shows "—." It's a pattern match on the title, not an AI judgment.
Where do manually added roles start on the board?

Every new role — whether from a job search or added by hand — starts in the "Sourced" column. A manually added role is also marked with source "manual" and no fit score ("—") until you run a match report. You move it forward yourself by dragging its card to another column.
Can I add a role without a link to the posting?

Yes — the link field in the "Add a role" modal is optional. Only "Company" and "Job title" are required. Without a link, you won't get automatic job-description loading in the role panel; you'd paste the JD text there yourself, and it saves to the card as you type.
I added a role with a posting link. Will CareerOps grab the job description for me?

Yes. Open the role panel — if there's a saved link and no JD yet, CareerOps calls its fetch function automatically. While it works, the hint reads "· loading job description…"; on success it becomes "· auto-loaded from the posting — edit if needed" and the text is saved to the card. You can edit it any time.
The JD didn't load automatically — what do I do?

If the fetch fails, the hint changes to "· couldn't auto-load — paste the JD here and it saves to the card automatically." Paste the job description text into the box yourself; it autosaves about 800 milliseconds after you stop typing, and the hint then reads "· saved to the card ✓."
I added a role with no link at all. How do I get the JD in?

Paste it directly into the JD box in the role panel. Since there's no URL on the card, the hint reads "· no link on this card — paste the JD here and it saves to the card automatically." Typing pauses for about 800 milliseconds before it autosaves, then the hint updates to "· saved to the card ✓."
I already pasted a JD on this role — will it try to reload it from the link?

No. If the role already has a saved job description, the panel just shows it with the hint "· saved with this card — edit if needed." CareerOps only calls the auto-fetch when there's a link but no JD saved yet. You can overwrite the saved text any time.
Does editing the job description text save automatically, or do I need to click something?

It autosaves — there's no save button for the JD box. Stop typing for about 800 milliseconds and CareerOps writes the text to the card; the hint then reads "· saved to the card ✓." That applies whether the JD arrived by auto-load, was pasted with no link, or is being edited afterward.
How do I see how well I match a role I'm tracking?

Open the role's panel and click "Get match report." It uses the JD already loaded on that card. While it runs, the button reads "Scoring…." If there's no JD saved yet, you'll see "Load or paste the job description first." — load or paste one, then try again.
Can I get a match score before I've added the role to my board?

Yes, from the standalone Résumé tool: paste the JD text into the tool and click "Get match report." It scores the same way as the role panel, but this run isn't saved anywhere — no card is updated and no report is stored, since there's no role for it to attach to.
My match report just says 'keyword overlap' — is that the real fit score?

No. That's the free, no-AI mode. Its sub-line says plainly "word-overlap scan, NOT a fit score" — it's counting matching words, not judging real fit. Missing terms show under "Missing keywords — add these to your résumé where true:" and matched ones under "Already covered:." No summary paragraph is shown.
What's the difference between the AI match report and the free one?

The free mode does a word-overlap scan and labels itself "keyword overlap." AI mode (free AI, Kimi K3, or Claude) instead gives a "recruiter-style assessment," labeled "scored on real fit, not word overlap," plus a written summary. Its labels are "Gaps to address (in your résumé or interview):" and "Your strongest evidence for this role:," with the standard AI warning underneath.
If I run a match report from the role panel, does it get saved?

Yes. Running it from the role panel writes the score onto the role as a percentage — so it shows on the card — and saves a report of kind "match" with the score and missing keywords. Reports run from the standalone Résumé tool are not saved this way.
The match report shows nothing under missing keywords. Is that a bug?

No — that means solid coverage. In keyword mode, an empty missing list shows "none — strong coverage." Run the same case from the standalone Résumé tool and the wording is slightly different: "none — great coverage." Either way, nothing obvious is missing against the JD you loaded.
Which AI actually generates my match report — do I get to pick?

The report names its source in the score note: "free AI," "Kimi K3," or "Claude," depending on what's active for your account. If you've saved both an Anthropic and a Kimi key, Claude runs it. With no keys saved, you're on the free tier, CareerOps's built-in free AI model.
I ran a match report on a role I already had a Jobscan report attached to — does it use that too?

No, not for the match report itself — Jobscan text only feeds into résumé tailoring, not into "Get match report." The match report always scores your stored résumé against the loaded JD, in keyword or AI mode depending on your tier, independent of anything attached from Jobscan.
How does the fit score I see on a card get there?

It's written automatically the first time you run "Get match report" from that role's panel — the score appears on the card as a percentage. Before that happens, the card shows "—" for fit score. Running the report again from the panel overwrites it with the newest score.
Does the match report tell me exactly what to change in my résumé?

It flags gaps, not rewrites. In AI mode you get "Gaps to address (in your résumé or interview):" plus a summary; in keyword mode you get "Missing keywords — add where true:." Either points you at what's missing against the JD — turning that into new résumé text is a separate step, using "✨ Tailor résumé."
How do I get an AI-tailored résumé for a specific role?

From the role panel, click "✨ Tailor résumé" (in the standalone Résumé tool it's "✨ AI rewrite résumé"). You need a job description loaded first — otherwise you'll see "Load or paste the job description first." While it runs, the button reads "✨ Working…," then the result appears in an editable textarea under a "Tailored résumé" heading.
Can I download the tailored résumé, or do I have to copy-paste it?

You can download it. Under the generated text you'll see "⬇︎ .txt" and "⬇︎ Word (.doc)" buttons (the standalone Résumé tool labels them "⬇︎ Download .txt" and "⬇︎ Download for Word (.doc)"). The .doc file is an HTML-based Word document styled in Calibri 11pt with your line breaks preserved.
I attached a Jobscan report to this role. Does tailoring my résumé actually use it?

Yes. If a Jobscan report is attached, up to the first 6,000 characters of its text are passed in automatically when you tailor your résumé. CareerOps flags this: "✨ Tailor résumé will close the exact gaps Jobscan flagged." The result's heading also gets " — built with your Jobscan findings" appended.
After I tailor my résumé, does it re-check my match score?

Yes, automatically. Once the tailored résumé is generated, CareerOps re-scores it against the same job description and updates the card. The note changes to something like "recruiter-style assessment of the tailored résumé — saved to the card," and the panel subtitle becomes "<Company> · <n>% match (tailored)."
How do I know which AI actually wrote my tailored résumé?

Check the heading above the generated text. It has " · free AI" or " · Kimi K3" appended to show which model ran it, alongside "Tailored résumé" (or "AI-tailored résumé" in the standalone tool). If a Jobscan report fed into it, " — built with your Jobscan findings" is added too.
Is there a way to make the tailored résumé sound less AI-written?

If you've saved an AI-Text-Humanizer login in Settings, a "✨ Humanize wording" button appears under the generated text — click it to rewrite the wording, and it shows "✨ Humanizing…" while running. Without credentials saved, that flow instead prompts "Add your AI-Text-Humanizer login in Settings first."
Do I need a job description loaded to tailor my résumé, or can I just use my stored one?

You need a JD. Résumé tailoring always compares your stored résumé against a specific job description — either the one saved on the role's card, or one you paste into the standalone Résumé tool. Without it, clicking "✨ Tailor résumé" returns "Load or paste the job description first."
If I tailor my résumé from the Résumé tool instead of a role panel, does it still save anything?

The output appears in the editable textarea for you to download, using the same underlying function and your stored résumé plus whichever JD you pasted. Use the role panel version instead if you want the run's score update saved onto a specific card — the standalone tool doesn't write anything back to a role.
Where's the button to generate a cover letter?

It's "✉️ Cover letter," available both in the role panel and the standalone Résumé tool. It calls the same underlying function as résumé tailoring, just in cover-letter mode. You need a job description loaded first, and the button shows "✨ Working…" while it generates.
Can I download my cover letter as a Word document?

Yes — the same download controls as résumé tailoring appear underneath: "⬇︎ .txt" and "⬇︎ Word (.doc)" (labeled "⬇︎ Download .txt" / "⬇︎ Download for Word (.doc)" in the standalone tool). The .doc download is an HTML file styled Calibri 11pt with your line breaks kept intact.
Does generating a cover letter show up anywhere on the role's card?

Yes. A generated cover letter is saved against the role as a report of kind "cover," which is what makes the 📄 icon appear on the card — its tooltip reads "tailored résumé/cover saved." That icon shows up whether the saved artifact is a tailored résumé, a cover letter, or both.
Will my cover letter use the Jobscan report I attached to this role?

Yes, the same way résumé tailoring does — if a Jobscan report is attached, up to the first 6,000 characters of its text are passed in when you generate the cover letter, and the output heading gets " — built with your Jobscan findings" appended.
How can I tell which AI model wrote my cover letter?
Look at the heading above the generated text — "Cover letter" with " · free AI" or " · Kimi K3" appended to show which model produced it, the same convention used for tailored résumés.
How do I start a chat with the AI about my job search?

Click "💬 Chat" in the header — it opens the "💬 Job-search chat" modal. Type in the textarea (placeholder: "Ask anything about your job search…") and press Enter to send, or Shift+Enter for a new line. The Send button shows "…" while working, and replies are labeled with the model that answered.
Does the chat know my résumé already, or do I have to paste it in?

It already knows it. The chat description says it "knows your résumé — ask it to tailor a bullet, draft outreach, prep an interview, whatever." You don't need to paste résumé text into the conversation; just ask directly.
I sent a chat message and got nothing back — did it fail?

If the model returns an empty reply, CareerOps shows "Model returned nothing — hit Send again." and restores your original text into the input box so you don't have to retype it. Just click Send again to retry.
Is my chat history saved for next time I open it?

No — chat history is kept only in memory for that session. There's no chat persistence, so closing the modal or reloading the app clears the conversation. Each time you open "💬 Chat" you're starting a fresh transcript.
Who's actually answering me in the chat — is it always the same model?

Messages are labeled with whichever model handled them: "CareerOps AI" on the free tier, or "Kimi K3" / "Claude" if you've saved your own key for one of those. Your own messages are labeled "You:." The standard AI warning appears below the transcript once there are messages.
How many free AI actions do I get per day?

Thirty — the free tier covers fit scoring, résumé tailoring, cover letters, and chat, all without a key, at 30 uses per day per person. Settings shows your live count as "<remaining> of <cap> free uses left today." The count resets at midnight.
I hit my daily AI limit — what happens now?

Every AI surface shows the same message: "You've used your 30 free AI actions today — resets at midnight. Add your own Anthropic or Kimi K3 key in Settings for unlimited use." Wait for the reset, or add your own key in Settings to keep working today.
Free AI says it's unavailable, but I haven't hit my daily cap. What's going on?

That's a separate outage message, not the cap message: "Free AI is temporarily unavailable. Try again shortly, or add your own Anthropic or Kimi K3 key in Settings." It means the free-tier provider is down right now.
How do I add my own Anthropic key so I'm not capped at 30 a day?

In Settings, under the AI section, paste your key into "Anthropic (Claude) API key" — the hint there is "optional · starts with sk-ant-." Once saved, you're billed directly by Anthropic, cents per use, instead of drawing from the free tier's daily cap.
I have both a Claude key and a Kimi key saved. Which one actually runs?

Claude. The stated rule is: "If both are saved, Claude is used." To use Kimi K3 instead, remove your Anthropic key — typing "remove" in that field in Settings deletes it, while leaving a key field blank keeps whatever's currently saved.
Is my own API key ever shown anywhere, like in an export?

No. Your Anthropic and Kimi K3 keys are described as "yours and are never shown in exports." The "Export everything (JSON)" download explicitly strips API keys (and the humanizer password) before it's generated.
Does CareerOps train its AI on my résumé or job descriptions?

No. The free tier's description is explicit: "Your text is sent to the model provider to generate the answer; it is not used to train any AI model." That applies to fit scoring, résumé tailoring, cover letters, and chat on the free tier.
Is there a paid CareerOps subscription I should know about?

No — there's no subscription tier at all. The only money involved is an entirely voluntary donation link, "☕ Support CareerOps" (a second link elsewhere reads "☕ Support"), via Stripe. Donating is optional and has no effect on your AI tier or limits.
I saved my own key but Settings still says I'm on the free tier — why?

If a key is saved, Settings should say "You're using <Claude (your key) | Kimi K3 (your key)>, so the free tier isn't being used." If it still shows the free-tier usage box instead, the save likely didn't take — re-enter the key (it must start with "sk-ant-" for Anthropic) and save again.
What's the Kimi K3 key for, and where do I get one?

It's the second bring-your-own-key option, an alternative to Claude — saved in Settings under "Kimi K3 (Moonshot) API key," hinted "optional · alternative to Claude." Guidance in the app points you to platform.kimi.ai for the key itself; like the Anthropic option, it's billed directly to you, cents per use.
What does the 'Humanize wording' button actually do?

It rewrites AI-generated text — a tailored résumé or cover letter — through a separate humanizer service, using an "AI-Text-Humanizer" email and password you've saved in Settings. The button only appears once those credentials are saved, and shows "✨ Humanizing…" while it's working.
I clicked Humanize wording and got an error instead of a rewrite. Why?

You likely haven't saved humanizer credentials yet. Without them, that flow returns "Add your AI-Text-Humanizer login in Settings first." Save your AI-Text-Humanizer email and password in Settings, then the "✨ Humanize wording" button will actually run instead of erroring.
How do I get all my CareerOps data out in one file?

In Settings, under "Your data," click "⬇︎ Export everything (JSON)." It downloads "CareerOps_export.json" containing an export timestamp, your profile, all your roles, and all your reports. Your API keys and humanizer password are stripped out before the file is generated.
I just want a spreadsheet of my board, not everything. Is there an option for that?

Yes — "⬇︎ Export board (CSV)" in Settings under "Your data" downloads "CareerOps_board.csv" with the columns company, title, level, stage, fit_score, match_score, url, and created_at. It's a lighter export than the full JSON one and skips reports entirely.
Can I download just one tailored résumé instead of exporting everything?

Yes — per-document downloads live right in the role panel and the standalone Résumé tool, as "⬇︎ .txt" and "⬇︎ Word (.doc)" buttons under each generated result. Filenames from the role panel follow "<Name>_<Company>_<Resume|CoverLetter|JobscanReport>"; from the Résumé tool it's "<Name>_<Resume|CoverLetter>."
Why does the exported JSON not include my Anthropic key?

That's intentional — Settings explicitly strips your API keys and your humanizer password before generating "CareerOps_export.json," so they never appear in the download even though the rest of your profile, roles, and reports are included.
Where in the app do I even find the export buttons?

Open Settings and scroll to "Your data." The copy there reads, in part, "export everything anytime below for your records." You'll see two buttons: "⬇︎ Export everything (JSON)" for the full export and "⬇︎ Export board (CSV)" for just the board as a spreadsheet.
How do I run a job search to find new roles?

Click "🔍 Run job search" in the header. While it runs, the button reads "🔍 Searching…." It scans live career boards at "75+ companies" and, on success, tells you "Added <n> new role(s) matching your preferences (scanned <found> live matches)." New roles land in "Sourced."
I ran a search and it says nothing new was added. Did it fail?

No — that means it worked but found nothing new: "Scanned the boards — <found> roles matched your filters, all already on your board. Adjust keywords in Settings to widen." Broaden your target titles, keywords, seniority, or locations in Settings and try again.
How many times a day can I run a job search?

Ten. Past that, you'll see "Daily search limit reached (10/day) — resets tomorrow. Your board and all other tools keep working." The cap resets the next day, and nothing else in the app is affected while you wait.
Which companies does the job search actually scan?

It scans live career boards at more than 75 named companies. In onboarding you can open a collapsible section labeled "See exactly which companies we search" to see the full list before you run your first search.
How do I change what kind of roles the search looks for?

Update your search preferences in Settings — "Target job titles," "Keywords," "Seniority," and "Locations" (use "Remote" there for remote roles). The search uses these filters against the 75+ boards it scans, so narrowing or widening them changes what counts as a match.
Does the job search apply to jobs on my behalf?

No. It only finds and adds matching roles to your board in "Sourced" — it never submits an application. CareerOps is explicit that "the board tracks your search; you submit each application yourself," using the "Apply — Open JD ↗" link on each role.
What does the onboarding wizard actually ask me for?

Five numbered sections under "Let's set up your job search 👋": "1 · What roles are you after?" (target titles, keywords, seniority, locations), "2 · About you" (name, phone, LinkedIn, location), "3 · Paste your résumé," "4 · AI" (optional keys), and "5 · How you'll actually use it."
Can I skip onboarding and just look at the board first?

Yes — the wizard has a "Skip for now — explore the board →" button alongside "Finish setup →." Skipping just means you'll see the yellow setup banner later prompting you to finish, since match reports and AI tailoring need your résumé saved first.
Do I have to enter my phone number during onboarding?

The "2 · About you" section asks for it, alongside full name, LinkedIn URL, and where you're based, but nothing in the source marks these fields as strictly mandatory beyond what's asked on that step. Fill in what you're comfortable sharing and continue.
Where do I tell CareerOps I only want remote roles?

In onboarding step "1 · What roles are you after?," under "Locations," type "Remote" — the field's hint says to use that to include remote roles. The same field also exists later in Settings if you want to change it after onboarding.
Is there a sign-out option while I'm in the middle of onboarding?
Yes — the onboarding wizard has its own "Sign out" button in the wizard header, separate from finishing or skipping setup. You don't have to complete or skip the wizard just to sign out.
There's a yellow banner on my board telling me to finish setup — what triggers that?

It appears whenever onboarding is incomplete or you haven't saved a résumé yet: "📝 Finish your setup — add your résumé to unlock the free match reports and AI tailoring. Finish setup →." Click through it to pick up onboarding where you left off.
Why can't I get a match report on this role?

If you haven't saved a résumé yet, match reports and AI tailoring aren't available — that's what the yellow "📝 Finish your setup" banner above the board is telling you. Click "Finish setup →" and complete "3 · Paste your résumé" in onboarding, then try again.
What's the 'FOLLOW-UPS DUE' strip above my board?

It's an automatic reminder list, labeled "⏰ FOLLOW-UPS DUE," built from roles sitting in "Applied" or "Interview." Each one comes due 14 days after the role was created, showing as a pill with the company and a truncated title. Click a pill to open that role.
One of my follow-up pills is red — what does that mean?
Red means it's overdue — the pill shows "follow up now" instead of a date. Pills that aren't yet due show "by YYYY-MM-DD" instead. Either way, clicking the pill opens that role's panel directly.
Can I set a custom follow-up reminder for a role, like in three weeks instead of two?

No — there's no manual reminder or calendar setting in CareerOps beyond the automatic follow-up strip. It's fixed at 14 days after a role's creation date, and only applies to roles in "Applied" or "Interview."
Why isn't a role I applied to weeks ago showing up in follow-ups?

Follow-ups only track roles currently sitting in "Applied" or "Interview" — if you've since moved it to another stage, it drops out of the strip. Check which column the card is in; the 14-day clock is based on when the role was created, not when it moved stage.
How do I attach a Jobscan report to a role?

Open the role panel and expand "Have a Jobscan report for this job? Attach it." Either upload the PDF with "⬆︎ Upload PDF," or paste the report text into the textarea and click "Save pasted text." The panel also explains how to get the PDF: "On jobscan.co: open your report → Print → 'Save as PDF' → upload it here."
My Jobscan PDF upload failed with a size error. What's the limit?

15 MB maximum. If your file is bigger, you'll see "PDF too big (15 MB max)." — you'd need to re-save a smaller PDF from Jobscan, or paste the report text into the textarea option instead, which has no size ceiling.
I uploaded something that wasn't a PDF and it got rejected — why?

Only PDF files are accepted for this upload. A non-PDF gives you "That needs to be a PDF (on jobscan.co: Print → Save as PDF)." — export your Jobscan report as a PDF first, then upload that file instead.
My Jobscan report is really long — does CareerOps read the whole thing?

No — text is extracted from at most the first 20 pages of the PDF. If your report is longer than that, only the first 20 pages' worth of text is used. Combined with the 15 MB size cap, that's the ceiling on what one upload can contain.
Does attaching a Jobscan report update my match score automatically?

It can. If the extracted text contains a match rate, that percentage is written onto the card automatically, and the panel subtitle updates to "<Company> · <n>% match (Jobscan)." If no match rate is found, the card isn't updated that way.
What happens on screen while my Jobscan PDF is uploading?

You'll see two states in sequence: "⬆︎ Reading PDF…" while the text is being extracted, then "⬆︎ Uploading…" while it saves. Once it finishes, the report appears in the "Saved for this role:" list at the bottom of the panel.
Can I remove a Jobscan report after I've attached it?

Yes — in the "Saved for this role:" list, Jobscan rows have a "✕ Remove" control. Clicking it asks you to confirm: "Remove this Jobscan report? You can attach a fresh one after re-running Jobscan." Confirm, and you're free to attach a new one.
Why does CareerOps make me upload a PDF for Jobscan instead of just connecting to my Jobscan account?

Because Jobscan has no public API to connect to — onboarding explains that directly, adding that "the built-in match report does the same job" as an alternative. The PDF-upload flow is the workaround for that missing integration, not a limitation CareerOps chose on its own.
Where can I see everything I've generated for a specific role?

At the bottom of the role panel, under "Saved for this role:." It lists every prior artifact by type — match report, tailored résumé, cover letter, Jobscan report — each with its date and match percentage where one applies.
What can I do with an item in the 'Saved for this role' list besides look at it?

Each row has controls: "View" to open it, "⬇︎ .doc" to download it as a Word file, and "Open PDF ↗" for a signed link that expires after 5 minutes. Jobscan rows additionally get a "✕ Remove" control to delete that report.
The 'Open PDF' link for my Jobscan report stopped working. Why?
Those links are signed URLs that expire after 5 minutes for security. Go back to the "Saved for this role:" list in the role panel and click "Open PDF ↗" again to generate a fresh link.
Does clicking Apply in CareerOps submit my application for me?

No. The green "Apply — Open JD ↗" link in the role panel just opens the real job posting in a new tab. CareerOps says it plainly: "The board tracks your search; you submit each application yourself" — there's no auto-apply feature.
Is there an auto-apply or LinkedIn Easy Apply integration planned?

Not in this app — onboarding states that LinkedIn integration and auto-apply are explicitly out of scope for the hosted tool. CareerOps focuses on tracking, scoring, and tailoring; actually submitting each application happens on the real posting, in your own hands.
What are the password requirements for creating a CareerOps account?

At least 8 characters — the password field's placeholder even says "8+ characters." If you try something shorter, you'll get "Password must be at least 8 characters." You can sign up with email and password, or use "Continue with Google" instead.
I signed up but can't sign in yet. What am I missing?

Check your email — after signup without an active session, CareerOps shows "Account created. Check your email to confirm, then sign in." Confirm via that email first, then return to the sign-in card and enter your credentials.
I forgot my password. How do I reset it?

Click "Forgot password?" on the sign-in card. CareerOps sends a reset link and shows "Password reset link sent — check your email." Follow that link to a "Set a new password" card, enter it under "New password," and click "Save new password."
I tried Continue with Google and it didn't work. What's wrong?

If Google sign-in isn't configured yet for your instance, you'll see "Google sign-in is being set up — use email/password for now." Use the email and password fields instead, or "Create account" if you don't have one yet.
What exactly is on the sign-in screen?

An "Email" field, a "Password" field (placeholder "8+ characters"), "Sign in" and "Create account" buttons, a "Forgot password?" link, a divider labeled "or," and "Continue with Google." The screen describes CareerOps as "Your private, automated job search."
Is my CareerOps data actually private, or can support staff see it?

The app states it directly under Settings → "Your data": "This is your data — private and yours alone. We can't access it." It also reminds you to export your data before you stop using the tool, since inactive accounts may eventually be cleaned up.
Where do I update my target job titles after onboarding?

Open the "⚙︎ Settings" button in the header. The modal's first fields are "Target job titles," "Keywords," "Seniority," and "Locations" — the same search preferences from onboarding step one. Update them and click "Save."
What sections does the Settings modal actually have?

In order: your search preferences (Target job titles, Keywords, Seniority, Locations), "Résumé," an "AI" section (free-tier usage box plus your Anthropic/Kimi key fields and humanizer email/password), and "Your data" with the export buttons. "Save" and "Close" buttons sit at the bottom.
If I close Settings without clicking Save, do my changes stick?

The modal has separate "Save" and "Close" buttons, which implies changes only persist once you click "Save" — closing without saving isn't documented as preserving edits. To be safe, always click "Save" before closing if you've changed anything.
How many columns does the CareerOps board have?

Seven: "Sourced," "Researched," "Conversation," "Applied," "Interview," "Offer," and "Rejected." They render left to right in that order, and each column header shows a count of cards in small uppercase type — an empty column just shows "—."
How do I move a role from Sourced to the next stage?

Drag its card to the column you want — dragging over a column outlines it with a dashed border, and dropping moves the role there. That's the only way stage changes happen; there's no dropdown or automatic promotion between columns.
I clicked a card and it moved columns by itself — is that normal?

No — a card only changes columns when you drag and drop it. If a click immediately follows a drag, CareerOps suppresses that click so it doesn't also open the panel, but the stage change itself only ever comes from the drag.
Does CareerOps ever move a role forward automatically, like after I run a match report?

No. The stage only changes when you drag a card to another column yourself — running a match report, tailoring a résumé, or attaching a Jobscan report updates the card's score or icon, never its stage.
What counts toward the 'active roles' number in my status line?

The status line reads "<N> active roles · click a card to scan / tailor / apply · drag between columns." That count excludes roles in "Rejected" — once you drag a card there, it stops counting toward your active total.
Is there a way to see the reason a role moved stages later?

Not as a visible note on the card — every drag-and-drop move writes to the database and logs a "stage_move" event behind the scenes, but there's no in-app view of that history. The board only ever shows the role's current column.
What do the stage names like 'Researched' or 'Conversation' actually mean?

CareerOps doesn't define that for you — there's no in-app description of what "Researched" or "Conversation" means versus any other stage beyond its label. You decide what each column represents for your own process and drag cards accordingly.
What's shown on each role's card on the board?

The job title in bold on the first line, then a meta line with a small badge for the fit score (or "—" if none), the company name, and "· <score> match" if a match score exists. A 📄 icon appears if a tailored résumé or cover letter is saved.
What does the little document icon on some cards mean?

That's the 📄 icon — its tooltip reads "tailored résumé/cover saved." It shows up once you've generated and saved at least a tailored résumé or a cover letter for that role, so you can spot at a glance which cards have artifacts ready.
Why does one of my cards have an 'Open JD' link and another doesn't?

The "Open JD ↗" link only appears on cards that have a URL saved — it opens the real posting in a new tab. A role added without a link, or where you only pasted JD text, won't show that link since there's no URL to open.
How do I search or filter my board for a specific company?

You can't — there's no board search box or filter controls in CareerOps. The board shows all your roles grouped into their seven columns; scanning visually or scrolling is the only way to find a specific card right now.
Can I sort the cards in a column by date added or match score?
No — there's no sort control on the board. Cards appear in whatever order the app renders them in each column; there's no way to reorder by date, score, or any other field.
Is there a notes field where I can jot down interview prep for a role?
No — there's no free-text notes field on a role. Anything you want to remember about a role has to live somewhere else; CareerOps doesn't offer an in-app notes box on the card or panel.
Can I tag or label roles, like 'dream job' or 'backup option'?
No — there are no tags or labels anywhere in CareerOps. The only categorization a role has is which of the seven board columns it's sitting in.
Does CareerOps have a dark mode?
No — dark mode isn't part of the app. The interface only has its one styling; there's no toggle for it in Settings or anywhere else.
Can I invite a friend or mentor to see my board?
No — there's no team or sharing feature. Your board, résumé, and reports are visible only to you, per your own signed-in account; nothing in CareerOps supports giving another person access to it.
